ground_truth.title: Data Science Weekly - Issue 630
ground_truth.author: Data Science Weekly
ground_truth.main_content:
Issue #630 of the Data Science Weekly newsletter featuring curated news, articles and jobs related to Data Science, AI, & Machine Learning.

Editor's Picks includes: 'Broken Chart: discover 9 visualization alternatives' discussing alternatives for presenting temperature data; 'Why You Should Read More Engineering Blogs' about learning from real-world engineering stories; and 'The Most Useful, Timeless Skill to Learn as a Data Professional' exploring leverage points in data careers.

This Week's Poll section invites reader engagement. Last Week's Poll shows results with a visualization. The newsletter features 'Probably Overthinking It: Use Data to Answer Questions and Make Better Decisions' by Allen B. Downey, author of Think Python, Think Bayes, and Think Stats, now in paperback. The book offers guidance on thinking clearly about data and making better decisions in an uncertain world.

Data Science Articles & Videos section curates links including: Statistical Learning Theory and Chat-GPT; 'My small data pipeline checklist'; MIT's Missing Semester 2026; Spherical Equivariant Graph Transformers; LLM evaluation in R; and articles on ring buffers, data management, dimensionality reduction, and AI agents.

Last Week's Newsletter's 3 Most Clicked Links featured 'The Girl Named Florida', 'How Google Maps quietly allocates survival across London's restaurants', and 'Traditional ML is dead' Reddit discussion.

Cutting Room Floor includes additional resources on AI agent evaluation, visual workflow automation, computational fluid dynamics, JAX for the web, and R Consortium's 2025 review.

The newsletter concludes with promotion for a 'Get A Data Science Job' course and advertising opportunities reaching ~68,750 subscribers with 30-35% weekly open rate, signed off by Hannah & Sebastian.
with: Once a week, we write this email to share the links we thought were worth sharing in the Data Science, ML, AI, Data Visualization, and ML/Data Engineering worlds.
Featured Book: PROBABLY OVERTHINKING IT - Use Data to Answer Questions and Make Better Decisions by Allen B. Downey.
Now in Paperback! Think more clearly about data, avoid common statistical pitfalls, and make better decisions in an uncertain world.
Looking to get a job? Check out our 'Get A Data Science Job' Course - a comprehensive course covering getting started, portfolio assembly, and resume writing.
Promote yourself/organization to ~68,750 subscribers by sponsoring this newsletter. 30-35% weekly open rate.
